requirements: Provide a 3U CubeSat primary frame with deployable rail guide grooves and PC/104 stack support.
Ensure structural integrity at corners with corner posts.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to different materials used in the structure.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies and conduct thermal cycling tests.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient stiffness at corner posts leading to structural failure.
risk.mit: Enhance mechanical stiffness at corner posts through additional reinforcements.

risk.desc: Possible misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances.
risk.mit: Refine manufacturing tolerances and conduct pre-assembly checks.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical shorts due to inadequate clearance around PC/104 standoffs.
risk.mit: Increase clearance around PC/104 standoffs and perform thorough electrical testing.

risk.desc: Potential difficulty in accessing internal components due to limited service access cutouts.
risk.mit: Expand service access cutouts to improve accessibility.

Post to provide structural integrity at corners

Perform connectivity tests to ensure proper electrical signal routing

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to lack of dedicated cooling path.

risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief geometry and follow minimum bend radius guidelines.

generated_parts.label: Payload Optics Bench
generated_parts.spec: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently

verify.rule: All required fields are present.
Mass estimate matches expected values.
All parts and features are traceable from initial design.
Interfaces match neighboring subsystem requirements.
Critical joints show visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex mating surfaces.
risk.mit: Implement strict alignment protocols and use precision tools.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion may affect optical performance over time.
risk.mit: Use materials with similar thermal expansion coefficients.

risk.desc: Limited space for harness routing may restrict flexibility.
risk.mit: Optimize harness layout and use modular connectors.

risk.desc: Inspection window may be prone to contamination.
risk.mit: Include protective covers and regular cleaning procedures.

risk.desc: Fasteners may loosen over time due to vibration.
risk.mit: Apply thread locker and monitor fastener integrity regularly.
